item.title: Three Ways to Get Paid 摘要
item.description:
Jason Zweig shares his father's three-part rule about making a living: lying to those who want to be lied to leads to riches; telling truth to truth-seekers provides a living; but telling truth to those who want lies leads to bankruptcy. The post pairs this with Om Malik's observation about the success of grifters and hucksters in today's world, suggesting a commentary on modern media and influencer culture where dishonesty often pays.
标签: ethics, truth, lying, media, influencers, society, wisdom, career, honesty, culture

item.title: California Brown Pelican 摘要
item.description:
The author is attending the Microsoft Build conference at Fort Mason in San Francisco, noting the presence of California Brown Pelicans diving in the water behind the venue. The post includes tags related to Microsoft, AI, generative AI, LLMs, and LLM releases.
标签: microsoft, ai, generative-ai, llms, llm-release, conference, san-francisco, wildlife

item.title: An Ode to the Exacting Pedantry of Computers 摘要
item.description:
The author reflects on how early programming required specifying number types (integers, floats, doubles), which felt pedantic and led them to drop a C++ class. Later, Python's dynamic typing felt more intuitive. Over time, they appreciated that computers' exacting precision forced careful thinking and anticipation of edge cases, improving design. With LLMs generating code from vague intent, that friction disappears, allowing faster output but risking half-conceived ideas. The author argues that articulating intent through code is essential for shaping good software, and losing that pedantry may lead to assumptions that users discover as bugs.
标签: programming, LLM, software engineering, type systems, developer experience, AI-assisted coding, critical thinking, code quality, pedagogy, software design

item.title: Scott Pelley Accuses CBS News Boss of ‘Murdering’ ‘60 Minutes’ 摘要
item.description:
Scott Pelley, a '60 Minutes' correspondent, fiercely criticized new executive producer Nick Bilton and editor-in-chief Bari Weiss during a staff meeting, accusing Weiss of 'murdering' the show. Pelley questioned Bilton's qualifications and the network's commitment to the program, following a major shake-up that included firing the previous executive producer and two correspondents. The turmoil comes despite '60 Minutes' winning two Emmys, a 9% ratings increase, and doubled digital views, indicating the show is thriving.
标签: CBS News, 60 Minutes, Scott Pelley, Bari Weiss, Nick Bilton, media turmoil, newsroom conflict, TV ratings, journalism, media management

item.title: The Metaverse Was Snake Oil for Isolation 摘要
item.description:
John Gruber argues that metaverse hype exactly coincided with Covid lockdown-induced isolation, not just roughly overlapping. He suggests the pandemic created a desperate need for technology that could substitute for real-world socializing, making people susceptible to the 'snake oil' promise of the metaverse. Gruber notes that extended lockdowns were only possible because modern technology like Zoom allowed a semblance of normalcy, but ultimately proved insufficient for education, work, and social connection. He questions whether metaverse hype would have occurred without Covid, though Facebook might still have rebranded due to brand toxicity.
标签: metaverse, Covid-19, lockdown, isolation, technology, socializing, Zoom, Facebook, snake oil, pandemic

item.title: Apple, the Anti-‘Metaverse’ VR Company 摘要
item.description:
John Gruber comments on the metaverse hype, noting that Apple never endorsed the term. At a 2022 WSJ event, Apple executives Greg Joswiak and Craig Federighi were asked to finish the sentence 'The metaverse is...' and Joswiak replied 'A word I'll never use.' Gruber contrasts Apple's stance with other companies and reiterates his belief that Apple's Vision platform is in the early stages of a long-term play, distinct from the metaverse fever dream.
标签: Apple, metaverse, VR, Vision Pro, Greg Joswiak, Craig Federighi, Daring Fireball, tech commentary, AR/VR, hype

item.title: Meta Reportedly Has a Slew of New Smart Glasses Planned for This Year 摘要
item.description:
Meta has ambitious hardware plans for 2026, including multiple smart glasses releases. According to The Information, two models are slated for December (codenamed 'Mojito VIP'), and two prototypes ('Artemis' and 'SSG' for 'supersensing glasses') will be tested in the fall. The SSG glasses feature always-on cameras for ambient visual assistance, like remembering where you left keys. Critics note Meta's pivot from the metaverse to real-world tracking devices, raising privacy concerns.
标签: Meta, smart glasses, hardware, AR, privacy, wearables, AI assistant, surveillance, tech news

item.title: Microsoft's new MAI models 摘要
item.description:
Microsoft announced two new text LLMs: MAI-Thinking-1 (35B parameters, reasoning-focused, available to select partners) and MAI-Code-1-Flash (5B parameters, for GitHub Copilot and VS Code). Both models are trained on clean, commercially licensed data without distillation from third-party models. MAI-Thinking-1 is claimed to be preferred over Sonnet 4.6 in blind evaluations. The low parameter counts are notable given the cost of larger models. The use of appropriately licensed data raises questions about whether these are the first useful code models not trained on unlicensed web data.
标签: AI, LLM, Microsoft, MAI-Thinking-1, MAI-Code-1-Flash, code generation, reasoning model, open source, licensing, GitHub Copilot
